config.general_notes: This table tracks radiopharmaceutical products (diagnostic, therapeutic and theranostic) in development that could compete with Ratio Therapeutics over the next 1–5 years. The list focuses on agents in pre-registration through Phase 1 and likely to reach market in 2024-2030. Your key goal is to monitor this competitive landscape. Please use the most recent information available from public sources, including clinicaltrials.gov, company press releases, and news articles.

validation_targets.description: Official code or INN of the radiopharmaceutical
Lead company (add parent in parentheses if acquired)
Molecular target or receptor the agent binds
validation_targets.notes: Use gene/protein symbol or common receptor acronym

validation_targets.description: Main disease(s) or tumour type(s) the product is being developed for
validation_targets.notes: Concise but specific (e.g., include biomarker status)

validation_targets.description: Radionuclide used for therapy (if applicable) for this product
Radionuclide used for imaging (if theranostic or diagnostic-only) for this product

validation_targets.notes: Include molecule class if helpful (peptide, mAb, small molecule)
If multiple, list the most advanced
Infer from typical timelines if not public

validation_targets.description: Notable regulatory designations (BTD, Fast Track, Orphan-Drug)
validation_targets.notes: List agency and year; '-' if none
Include major CDMO / isotope-supply partners if relevant

validation_targets.description: Latest important news on the product development (includes date of information release)
validation_targets.notes:
Bullets with aggregated news on a product. Confidence is only assesed for new news that is to be added - all other data is past forward. Include past news and append new news to the beginning of the list. Starts with a dash, date: news (confidence level). Use 
 to separate each line.
examples:
- 4/10/2025: BLA resubmission in Q1 2025 after OS update requested (High Confidence)
- 3/10/2025: Phase 3 trial results expected in Q2 2025 (High Confidence)
- 2/10/2025: Phase 3 trial initiated (High Confidence)
- 1/10/2025: Phase 2 trial results expected in Q4 2025 (High Confidence)

rows.Recent News:
- 05/15/2024: First patient dosed in Phase 1 study (High Confidence)
- 02/10/2024: IND cleared by FDA (High Confidence)
- 01/15/2024: Preclinical data presented at conference showing promising results in animal models (Medium Confidence)

- 04/20/2024: Interim Phase 2 results show 60% response rate (High Confidence)
- 12/05/2023: Fast Track designation granted by FDA (High Confidence)

rows._row_key: Granzyme B PET Tracer||Ratio Therapeutics (licensed from Merck)||Granzyme B
rows.Recent News: - 03/07/2024: License agreement signed with Merck for technology development (High Confidence)

Development Stage.quote: RTX-1363S is currently in preclinical development at Ratio Therapeutics.
Ratio Therapeutics has completed IND-enabling studies for RTX-1363S and expects to file with the FDA in Q1 2024.
Ratio Therapeutics and Lantheus announced today that the first patient has been dosed in a Phase 1 study of [^64Cu]RTX-1363S in patients with solid tumors.

Indication.quote: Ratio is developing RTX-1363S for potential use in various solid tumors that express FAP.
The program is initially focused on solid tumors with high FAP expression, including pancreatic, colorectal, and breast cancers.

Key Trial ID.quote: No clinical trials have been registered yet for this agent.
A Phase 1 Study of [^64Cu]RTX-1363S PET/CT in Patients With Solid Tumors - NCT05760989

Development Stage.quote: Ratio Therapeutics is exploring Ac-225 labeled compounds against various targets including FAP.
Preliminary animal studies with FAP-targeted Ac-225 agents are showing promising tumor targeting and therapeutic efficacy.

Indication.quote: FAP is expressed across multiple solid tumor types, making it an attractive target for radiotherapeutics.
Initial preclinical work is focusing on pancreatic cancer models, where FAP expression is particularly high in the tumor stroma.

Development Stage.quote: Ratio Therapeutics and Novartis initiate Phase 1 clinical trial of novel SSTR2-targeted radiotherapeutic.
Following positive Phase 1 results, Ratio and Novartis have advanced their SSTR2-targeted radiotherapeutic into Phase 2 studies.

Indication.quote: The Phase 1 trial is enrolling patients with advanced or metastatic neuroendocrine tumors expressing SSTR2.
Phase 2 study is focusing specifically on gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (GEP-NETs) based on promising activity observed in this population during Phase 1.

FDA-EMA Designation.quote: No special regulatory designations have been granted for this compound yet.
The FDA has granted Fast Track designation to the SSTR2-targeted radiotherapeutic being developed by Ratio Therapeutics and Novartis for the treatment of advanced GEP-NETs.
